transcript: If you're a beginner, you might be wondering, how do I dribble past someone? And what skill moves are the best? So that's why today, we'll show you the 15 best and most simple skills in the entire game. And we've ordered them by level of difficulty, with number 1 being the easiest skill to master, and number 15 being the hardest you're watching all attack change of speed one of the most simple and effective skills in all of football is really barely a skill at all in fact all you really do is push the ball past the defender and run. Cuts. Perhaps the most common skill in all of football. Dribble at a defender. Then, before you get too close, use the inside or outside of your foot to cut the ball at an angle past the defender the underfoot stop When you're dribbling down the field And a defender is catching up with you Use the underside of your foot to stop the ball And now you have more time and space To decide what you want to do. The Shoulder Drop When you're facing a a defender take a big step one direction leaning your body and dropping your shoulder that way then use your other foot to push the ball the other way the Cruyff When a defender is moving to block your shot or cross, wind up like you're going to shoot, But instead, use the inside of your foot to drag the ball behind your leg. Fake kick. Run up to the ball and wind up like you're going to shoot pass or cross but instead don't and push the ball a different way the stop andgo underside of foot when you're dribbling down the field but a defender is catching up with you use the underside of your foot to stop the ball then immediately use the inside of your other foot to push the ball past the defender. The stop-and-go inside a foot. The stop-and-go with the inside of your foot is almost exactly the same as the stop-and-go with the underside of your Thank you. The stop-and-go inside a foot. The stop-and-go with the inside of your foot is almost exactly the same as the stop-and-go with the underside of your foot. The only difference is that you use the inside of your foot to stop the ball before pushing it past the defender. When a defender reaches for the ball, use the underside of your foot to pull the ball back, then use the inside of that same foot to push the ball away from the defender. 1, out. Use the inside of your foot and start dragging the ball across your body. Then use the outside of that same foot to cut the ball the other way. La Croqueta. Use the inside of your foot and slide the ball across your body to your other foot. Then use the inside of your other foot to push the ball past the defender. The pull behind leg. When a defender is coming in from the side, use the underside of your foot to pull the ball back and behind your leg. Then use the inside of that same foot to push the ball behind your leg and away from the defender. the inside step over when a defender is on your back use one foot and step across your body around the front of the ball then use the outside of that same foot to cut the ball the other way. The step over. When you're dribbling at a defender, bring one foot around the front of the ball. Then use the top of your other foot to push the ball the other direction, past the defender. The Double Cut When you're dribbling at a defender, use the top of your foot to make a small cut one direction. Then quickly use the inside of that same foot to cut the ball back the other way. all right everyone we hope you liked that video if you did make sure to leave a thumbs up right down there make sure to subscribe and if you haven't already make sure to check out our other videos if you want to learn more about football.
